summary: McKenzie recommends Aaron Wall's Search Value Pyramid as the single most important resource for understanding niche marketing. He illustrates the concept with his own experience: losing rankings on broad, generic terms like 'bingo cards' matters less than gaining rankings on specific, high-intent search queries like 'need bingo cards for reading class.' The post is a brief but pointed reminder that search traffic quality matters far more than quantity.
key_points: The Search Value Pyramid from SEOBook is essential reading for anyone doing niche marketing
Generic high-volume search terms drive traffic but not necessarily revenue
Specific, long-tail search queries with clear purchase intent are far more valuable
Losing rank on vanity keywords while gaining rank on buyer-intent keywords is a net win
money_quotes: It's so true it hurts, as I have to keep reminding myself every time I slip another place on [bingo cards] (oh no, my traffic numbers!) and gain another place on [need bingo cards for reading class] (oh yes, my wallet!)
key_insight: A single ranking gain on a specific, high-intent search query is worth more to your business than ranking on a broad, high-traffic keyword.
